ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311069 covers rice noodle roll as a closest parent; no specific vegetarian Vietnamese steamed rice roll entry exists in FoodOn as of 2026-04.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX used as closest parent for steamed rice-based dish; no specific code for Vietnamese vegetarian steamed rice roll with mock pork exists in FoodEx2 as of 2026-04.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese vegetarian steamed rice roll dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 280 g, comprising two to three steamed rice rolls filled with sliced vegetarian mock pork roll, served with dipping sauce on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Banh Uot (Steamed Rice Roll) — component-based estimation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Rice flour (ID 20068), Tofu/soy protein analogue (ID 16126), and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia (2nd ed.) reference values for steamed rice-based dishes
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Bánh ướt chả lụa chay exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables. Values estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 60% steamed rice flour sheet (per USDA SR Legacy rice flour, cooked/steamed adjustment), 25% vegetarian mock pork roll (soy protein analogue, USDA SR Legacy tofu/soy analogue), 10% sauce/seasoning (soy sauce, sesame oil), 5% garnish (spring onion, mushroom). Macros cross-checked against published Vietnamese food composition data (Nguyen et al., 2007, Vietnam Food Composition Table, National Institute of Nutrition).

gi_source.title: Rice noodle / steamed rice sheet GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bánh ướt chả lụa chay. GI of 68 estimated from closest analogous food: fresh rice noodles / steamed rice sheet (GI ~68, medium category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables and the University of Sydney GI Database. The vegetarian filling (soy protein, mushroom) has minimal glycemic impact; the dominant glycemic driver is the steamed rice flour sheet. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 54.6) / 100 ≈ 37.1, rounded to 36.6 using 53.9 g carbohydrate in 280 g serving (19.5 g/100g × 2.8).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Each 280 g serving provides approximately 868 mg sodium, representing 38% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce or consume a smaller portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index from steamed rice flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 68 (medium) and a glycemic load of ~37 per standard serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a plate and pair with non-starchy vegetables or additional protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and zero cholesterol from plant-based ingredients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is cholesterol-free and low in saturated fat (0.5 g/100g), making it a relatively heart-friendly option; however, sodium from soy-based seasoning should be monitored in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh ướt chả lụa chay is a Vietnamese vegetarian steamed rice roll dish served in Singapore at Vietnamese restaurants catering to Buddhist and plant-based diners. The dish consists of thin, silky steamed rice flour sheets (bánh ướt) rolled around slices of vegetarian mock pork roll (chả lụa chay), which is typically made from seasoned soy protein, mushroom, and starch, shaped and steamed to mimic the texture of traditional Vietnamese pork sausage. The rolls are served on a plate, drizzled with a light soy-based dipping sauce and sesame oil, and garnished with fried shallots and spring onion. The texture is soft, smooth, and slightly chewy from the rice flour, with a savoury, umami-rich filling. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in carbohydrates (approximately 19.5 g/100g) from the rice flour base, provides modest plant-based protein (approximately 4.8 g/100g) from the soy-based mock pork, and is low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free. Sodium is the primary clinical concern at approximately 310 mg/100g due to soy sauce seasoning. The glycemic index is estimated at 68 (medium), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, and is generally halal-friendly. It is commonly consumed as a light breakfast or lunch in Vietnamese culinary tradition and is available in Singapore's Vietnamese restaurant scene.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Bánh ướt chả lụa chay was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis of rice flour (steamed) and soy protein analogue using USDA SR Legacy data, cross-referenced with the Vietnam National Institute of Nutrition Food Composition Table. GI is an analogous estimate from steamed rice noodle/sheet data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach rather than a direct food match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no direct laboratory analysis or exact database entry was found for this specific dish. Values should be verified against laboratory analysis or a future HPB Singapore Foodo entry if one becomes available. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats in standard preparation). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — entirely plant-based ingredients). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no animal-derived ingredients; plant-based soy analogue does not naturally contain B12 unless fortified). 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources used for this estimation. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. 4. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from steamed rice noodle/sheet data; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found. 5. The dish is classified as Vietnamese cuisine served in Singapore; multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) plus Vietnamese as the dish's origin language. 6. Serving size of 280 g is estimated based on typical Vietnamese restaurant portion for this dish type. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g values are estimated from the fatty acid profile of sesame oil and soy protein analogue components.
